summary: Cleaned up Ness motion-state table definitions by replacing raw hexadecimal motion flags and move-id bitfields with named constants and expressions. The PR added reusable ftNs_MF_* flag combinations to ftNess/forward.h, updated ftNs_Init_MotionStateTable to use those constants plus FtMoveId_* shifts, and renamed two generic FtMoveId enum entries to AttackHi4 and AttackLw4.
what_changed: Added #include "forward.h" to ftNs_Init.c so the Ness-specific motion flag constants are available to the motion-state table.
Introduced a set of static u32 const ftNs_MF_* ATTRIBUTE_USED constants in src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/forward.h.

Factored repeated motion flag bitmasks into named combinations such as ftNs_MF_Attack4, ftNs_MF_AttackHi4, ftNs_MF_AttackLw4, ftNs_MF_Special, ftNs_MF_SpecialN, ftNs_MF_SpecialAirN, ftNs_MF_SpecialLwLoop, and ftNs_MF_SpecialAirLwLoop.
Replaced raw motion flags like 0x00241A09, 0x00340111, 0x00340513, and 0x003C0414 in ftNs_Init_MotionStateTable with the new ftNs_MF_* constants.

Replaced raw move-id fields like 0x0A000000, 0x0B800000, 0x0C000000, 0x12000000, and 0x15000000 with FtMoveId_* << 24 expressions, including the explicit charge/start bit expression (1 << 23) where present.
Renamed FtMoveId_Unk11 to FtMoveId_AttackHi4 and FtMoveId_Unk12 to FtMoveId_AttackLw4 in src/melee/ft/forward.h.
smart_moves: Grouped common flag subsets hierarchically instead of defining every motion state's full flag word independently; for example, aerial special variants reuse ground special constants plus FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol.
Kept the original encoded semantics visible by expressing move IDs as shifts rather than leaving opaque high-byte hex constants.
Used ATTRIBUTE_USED on header-level static constants, which helps avoid unused-constant issues while allowing constants to live near the Ness motion-state enum definitions.
Improved cross-fighter naming in the global FtMoveId enum by converting two unknown move IDs into meaningful smash attack names.
decomp_lessons: When motion-state tables contain repeated hexadecimal flags, decode them into named FtStateChange_* combinations before doing larger behavioral work; this makes future audits and matching changes safer.
Move-id fields in MotionState entries can be represented as FtMoveId_* << 24, with additional bits ORed explicitly when needed, instead of hard-coded packed constants.
Character-local forward headers are a reasonable home for fighter-specific motion flag aliases when those aliases are needed by init tables and align with the fighter's motion-state enum.
Decode enum placeholders such as FtMoveId_Unk11 only when the table evidence supports the name; here the Ness AttackHi4/AttackLw4 entries directly justify the rename.
naming_conventions: Ness-specific motion flag constants use the prefix ftNs_MF_ followed by the move or grouped behavior name, such as ftNs_MF_AttackS4 and ftNs_MF_SpecialAirHi.
Global move IDs use the FtMoveId_* prefix and canonical move names, e.g. FtMoveId_AttackHi4 and FtMoveId_AttackLw4.
Motion-state enum names continue to use the ftNs_MS_* pattern for Ness-specific states.
Flag constants mirror existing FtStateChange_* names rather than inventing new abbreviations for individual bits.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace packed hex constants with compile-time constant expressions only when they are expected to fold to the identical values.
Preserve exact bit packing for motion-state move IDs by using FtMoveId_* << 24 and retaining explicit ORed bits such as (1 << 23).
Use layered constant definitions to document shared bit patterns while still emitting the same immediate values in the motion-state table.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter MotionState tables still using raw hexadecimal FtStateChange flag words.
Search for remaining FtMoveId_Unk* entries that can be resolved from motion-state table evidence.
Compare other character forward.h files for established placement and naming of fighter-specific motion flag constants.
Audit uses of the high move-id field to confirm when additional bits like (1 << 23) represent charge/start state metadata.
